contacts.name: Dana Whitfield
contacts.notes: Former colleague from my last team. Offered to refer me at Atlas Forge.

contacts.name: Marcus Lee
contacts.notes: Reached out on LinkedIn. Responsive; runs the platform-team loop.

contacts.name: Priya Nair
contacts.notes: Hiring manager for the edge-routing team. Great conversations all the way through.

hardFilters.notes: Comp floor is base salary; flexible if equity and growth are strong. Remote-first only — open to occasional travel, but not hybrid or return-to-office.

softPreferences.notes: Core stack is Node/TypeScript and React with a Java services background; happy to go deeper on backend and developer platform. Fintech and payments are fine. Want a team that values testing, code review, and a sustainable on-call rotation.

differentiators: Scaled a Node/TypeScript API platform to 50M requests/day while cutting p99 latency 40%
Led the migration of a Java monolith to TypeScript services with zero customer downtime
Built and maintained a React design system adopted by 30+ engineers across six teams
Mentored five engineers from mid to senior and owned the team's code-review and testing standards
redFlagPatterns: Vague scope or "wear many hats" with no clear ownership
Unlimited PTO presented as a perk with no stated minimum
On-call with no compensation or rotation detail
Return-to-office creep dressed up as "flexible remote"

fitCalibration.digest: Senior IC backend, fintech/payments, $195–225k remote, Java/TypeScript
fitCalibration.note: Fintech and payments are a fine domain for me — don't down-rank a role just for being in it.

fitCalibration.digest: Senior IC backend, media/streaming, $200–230k remote, Node/TypeScript/React
fitCalibration.note: I want to stay an IC — a role that drifts toward people-management is a real downgrade for me.
